In the Name of Allâh, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
All the praises and thanks be to Allâh, the LordFootnote-1 of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists).Footnote-2
The Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
The Only Owner (and the Only Ruling Judge) of the Day of Recompense (i.e. the Day of Resurrection)
You (Alone) we worship, and You (Alone) we ask for help (for each and everything).
Guide us to the Straight Way.Footnote-3

The Way of those on whom You have bestowed Your GraceFootnote-4 , not (the way) of those who earned Your Anger Footnote-5 (i.e. those who knew the Truth, but did not follow it) nor of those who went astray (i.e. those who did not follow the Truth out of ignorance and error). Footnote-6, Footnote-7
